danger_signals.visual_form: Shield indicator bars decreasing, visual feedback around cockpit

danger_signals.meaning: Enemy has targeting lock; evasion required

danger_signals.visual_form: System readout changes (engine, weapons, hull status)

safety_signals.visual_form: Empty or minimal blips on radar display
Allied fighter blips on radar, wingmen visible in viewport
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical support available; reduced isolation risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Large contact on radar, visible structure ahead
System readout showing green/full status indicators

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: X-Wing fighter silhouette; alliance starbird insignia
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Rebel bases, Mon Calamari cruisers, asteroid bases

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red/orange warning coloration on imperial craft
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Imperial insignia; TIE fighter distinctive 'twin pod' silhouette
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: TIE variants (fighter, interceptor, bomber); Star Destroyers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Imperial Space Stations, planetary occupation markers, Star Destroyer fleet formations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple radar contacts, weapon system activity indicators, visual effects for laser fire and explosions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy fighters perform evasive maneuvers, attack patterns, coordinated squadron tactics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sparse radar, neutral/minimal threat indicators, clear space environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Wingmen maintain formation, scan for contacts, reduced alert posture

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Imperial stations visible, TIE patrols on radar, imposing Star Destroyer visual presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Imperial forces aggressive toward player; organized interception patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rebel bases visible, friendly capital ships present, reduced imperial presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Friendly forces provide tactical support; briefing intel reflects alliance priorities

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: System readout shows red/damaged sections, visual sparks/venting effects on ship exterior
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced maneuverability, slower response, potential system failures (weapons, shields, engines)

environmental_storytelling.example: Wreckage type (X-Wing vs TIE fighter) indicates which faction controlled area and recent conflict outcome
Imperial vs Rebel base designs differ visually; imperial stations feature stark geometry and red warning markers
Presence of capital ships (Mon Calamari cruisers = Rebel; Star Destroyers = Empire) signals territorial control
Mission briefing describes political situation (blockade, supply runs, reconnaissance); visuals confirm described scenario
Heavy imperial traffic in system indicates occupation/control; absence suggests rebel-held space

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Evasive maneuvers, counterattack, wingmen support called via radio
Aggressive pursuit, formation flying, coordinated fire patterns
Retreat toward friendly capital ship or base; reduced maneuverability
Maintains relative position, mirrors player banking/throttle, provides fire support
Slow transit, launches periodic TIE sweeps, appears as overwhelming force on approach
Maintains course, moves at constant velocity; becomes target priority if endangered

notes: X-Wing (1993) uses cockpit-centric visual design typical of space flight simulators. Primary world-state communication occurs through diegetic UI (instrument panels, radar, system readouts) rather than external visual effects. Faction identity is communicated through distinct starfighter silhouettes (X-Wing symmetry vs TIE twin-pod design), base architecture, and fleet composition rather than color-coding. The simulation paradigm emphasizes instrumental feedback (threat indicators, system status) over dramatic visual spectacle. Political situation emerges from mission briefings combined with encountered faction forces and environmental markers (controlled/contested territory indicators).
